summary.intent: Fix bugs identified in a Greptile review for MCP timeout configuration, then keep the branch clean by merging main and fixing formatter failures.
summary.outcome: All fixes were applied and pushed: is_alive logic corrected, timeout field renamed, main merged cleanly, and two formatter violations (biome + rustfmt) resolved and committed.

repo: The project runs parallel local checks: biome (JS formatting), rustfmt via `format-check` recipe, zizmor (GitHub Actions security), and i18n parity — all must pass before CI is clean.
Streamable HTTP MCP servers return non-text/event-stream content-type even when healthy; is_alive must accept any 2xx, not just text/event-stream responses.
Timeout config in manager.rs and page-mcp.js distinguishes between the configured value (applied on next connect) and the in-use value of running transports — naming must reflect this.

code.finding: rustfmt wants method-chain breaks differently: `SseTransport::with_auth_remote(...).unwrap()` must be split as `let transport =\n    SseTransport::with_auth_remote(...).unwrap();`
biome requires showToast call collapsed onto one line rather than split across multiple lines with separate string arguments.

workflow: Format checks are strict and enforced by a `format-check` just recipe; always run formatters before committing.
Background cargo check tasks complete asynchronously and post task-notification events — no need to block on them synchronously.
Merging main before pushing fixes avoids later rebase churn; this merge was conflict-free.

friction: Formatter failures (biome + rustfmt) were not caught before the initial push, requiring a follow-up fix commit.
Two separate formatter tools (biome for JS, rustfmt for Rust) with different style rules must both be satisfied independently.
